prompt: A DispatchOps Courier Operations & Settlement system with main features including courier fulfillment control, internal task collaboration, customer and courier credibility management, contract templating with local e-signatures, and offline-ready payments and reconciliation. Roles include Administrator, Operations Manager, Dispatcher, Courier, and Auditor. Using a jQuery-driven web UI, Dispatchers create and release delivery jobs, validate service areas before assignment, and generate pick lists and sort lists for a daily run; the interface shows fulfillment progress as clear status steps (Created, Picked, In Transit, Delivered, Exception) with color-coded warnings when a job is idle for more than 20 minutes. Operations staff work from To-do/Done/CC inboxes, assign tasks with due times, transition statuses with mandatory comments on “Blocked” or “Exception,” and use @mentions to request action; tasks can be linked to a delivery job and optionally placed on an internal calendar view for shift planning. Customers and Dispatchers rate completed deliveries across timeliness, attitude, and accuracy on a 1–5 scale and may file an appeal within 48 hours; couriers see their credit level (A–D) and the current acceptance limit (e.g., Level A up to 8 concurrent jobs, Level D limited to 1) along with any violations and penalty windows. Contract and document templates support placeholders and versioning; staff generate agreements locally, route them for signing, and see a tamper-evident signing record. Site-wide search provides fast lookup across titles, descriptions, tags, and authors, with filters, sorting, trending terms, and a “no results” prompt suggesting synonyms and related categories; profiles support structured edits, optional media uploads with basic format/size checks, and tiered visibility for private fields. The backend uses Spring MVC to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the jQuery frontend, with MyBatis mapping business entities into MySQL for durable, offline-first persistence. A unified payment API is implemented as a local payment gateway abstraction (cash, check, and internal account balance), enforcing signature verification for any recorded callbacks coming from connected on-prem devices and strict idempotency using a unique payment reference so retries cannot double-post; refunds are supported only against settled transactions within 30 days and must retain an immutable ledger trail. Reconciliation exports a local CSV report by date range with totals, adjustments, and per-transaction lines, and requires Auditor or Administrator permission. Delivery-area validation uses stored region rules and shipping templates (by state/ZIP range, weight in pounds, and order amount thresholds) and blocks dispatch if the address fails validation; fulfillment events are append-only for auditability. Notifications are implemented as a pluggable local subscription mechanism (in-app inbox and optional LAN message relay) with no dependency on SMS, email, or any external network service. Sensitive fields such as passwords and private profile data are protected with salted hashing and AES-at-rest encryption, and the system enforces role-based access, account lockout after 5 failed logins for 15 minutes, and full local operation with all files, signatures, and evidence stored on the organization’s own server disk.
